texto: All my life I have preserved in the depths of my heart a live faith in my Creator, the Defender of the World, in His Sanctifying Grace and in the expiatory sacrifice of Christ our Saviour, but never have I agreed that true religion demands outward manifestations.

frase_original: All my life I have preserved in the depths of my heart a live faith in my Creator, the Defender of the World, in His Sanctifying Grace and in the expiatory sacrifice of Christ our Saviour, but never have I agreed that true religion demands outward manifestations.

texto: Rare and powerful harmonies exist,Shaping both scent and contour in a flower.Thus brilliance lies unseen by us until,Beneath the chisel, it blazes in the diamond.And thus do images of fleeting vision,Drifting above like cloud-forms in the sky,Once turned to stone live on from age to age,Held always in a faultless, polished phrase.("A Sonnet To Form")
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Valery Bryusov: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Rare and powerful harmonies exist,Shaping both scent and contour in a flower.Thus brilliance lies…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, art, poem pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de poem e poetry.
